story_title: Strait of Hormuz / Project Freedom paused
story_one_liner: Twenty-seven countries reported the same Hormuz deal today. They all agree on the facts, and they all tell completely different stories. Can they all be telling the truth?
music_brief: ambient drone bed throughout; intro sting handles open

world_tickers.headline: Trump recua: operação em Hormuz suspensa
Japan to buy 20m more UAE oil bypassing Hormuz
US trade gap widens as oil exports surge
Lufthansa fuel bill jumps €1.7B on Iran war
Inflation hits 7.2% — first Asian domino to fall
National Bank raises rates 8.25% citing Hormuz
Pyongyang gasoline now pricier than Seoul's

scenes.voiceover: Five countries. One Iran deal. Same facts. Five very different stories.

Today, the United States and Iran reached a one-page memorandum to end the Hormuz war. Pakistan brokered the talks. Brent crude fell eleven percent. So far, so factual. But here's how the world reported it.
Wire services kept it neutral. "Trump pauses operation. Iran says safe passage possible." Reuters. Japan Times. Sydney Morning Herald. The baseline. Boring. Right?
scenes.on_screen_text:
🌐 WIRE: 'Trump pauses operation'
Reuters / Japan Times / SMH
scenes.headline_quoted: U.S. and Iran closing in on memorandum to end war — Japan Times

scenes.voiceover: The United States. Trump on Truth Social: "the legendary Epic Fury will be at an end. If they don't agree, the bombing starts at a much higher level than before." Triumph and threat in the same sentence.
scenes.on_screen_text:
🇺🇸 USA: 'The legendary Epic Fury will be at an end'
Trump, Truth Social
scenes.headline_quoted: Trump warns 'much higher-level' bombing will start if Iran refuses — Yonhap News

scenes.voiceover: China heard a different story. Foreign minister Wang Yi: "Beijing will play a greater role in restoring peace." Hong Kong Free Press credits Chinese diplomacy with the breakthrough. China isn't pausing — China is brokering.
scenes.on_screen_text:
🇨🇳 CHINA: 'Beijing will play a greater role'
Wang Yi, via HKFP
scenes.headline_quoted: Beijing to play 'greater role' in ending Mideast fighting — HKFP

scenes.voiceover: Iran heard victory. Iran International cites the Supreme Leader declaring the United States suffered, quote, "a shameful defeat." The same pause. Two superpowers, two winners.
scenes.on_screen_text:
🇮🇷 IRAN: 'A shameful defeat for the US'
Khamenei, via Iran International
scenes.headline_quoted: Iran president says US naval blockade 'doomed to fail' — Ahram Online

scenes.voiceover: And then the paradox. Foreign Affairs — the establishment magazine of US foreign policy — published this. Quote: Iran proved it can close the strait, even in the face of significant military force. The IRGC stays in place. The same conclusion as Russian state media.
scenes.headline_quoted: Iran's New Oil Weapon — Foreign Affairs

scenes.voiceover: Same facts. Five stories. All defensible. Could every one of them be telling the truth? Daily framings. Follow.

fact_check_provenance.primary_event_source: Pakistani diplomatic source via Reuters, confirmed by Axios

supporting_quotes_per_frame.exact_headline: U.S. and Iran closing in on memorandum to end war, sources say
Trump warns 'much higher-level' bombing will start if Iran does not agree to a deal
Beijing to play 'greater role' in ending Mideast fighting, Chinese FM says
Iran president says US naval blockade 'doomed to fail'
